description:
You are a support-desk engineer who has been asked to collect a small, self-contained “diagnostic bundle” for the fictitious service  “sample-app”.  
Everything you need has already been placed in the user’s home directory; your job is simply to gather the right pieces and package them exactly as requested.

1. Create a new directory named  
   /home/user/support_bundle  

2. Copy the following source artefacts into that directory, renaming each file exactly as shown:  
   • From /home/user/sample-app/config.yaml          ➔  config.yaml  
   • From /home/user/sample-app/logs/app.log         ➔  app.log  
   • From /home/user/sample-app/status.txt           ➔  service_status.txt  
   • From /home/user/sample-app/env_vars             ➔  env.txt  

   (Do NOT alter the contents of any file while copying.)

3. Inside /home/user/support_bundle create a text file called manifest.txt that lists the four files you just placed there.  
   Output-format requirements for manifest.txt:  
     a. Plain ASCII/UTF-8 text with UNIX (LF) line endings only.  
     b. Each line contains **only** the file’s base-name (no path, no checksum, no size, nothing else).  
     c. Lines must be sorted in strict lexicographical order.  
     d. Every line (including the last) must end with a single LF.  

   Therefore, manifest.txt must contain **exactly** the following four lines:  
        app.log  
        config.yaml  
        env.txt  
        service_status.txt  

4. Create a compressed archive named /home/user/support_bundle.tar.gz that contains **only** the five files located in /home/user/support_bundle (i.e. the four artefacts plus manifest.txt).  
   • Files must live at the root of the archive (no enclosing directory).  
   • Use GNU tar’s default ownership/permission behaviour—do not attempt to change modes or owners.

5. Leave the original /home/user/support_bundle directory in place alongside the new .tar.gz file.

The automated tests will verify ALL of the following:

• The directory /home/user/support_bundle exists.  
• The files app.log, config.yaml, env.txt and service_status.txt inside that directory are byte-for-byte identical to their originals.  
• manifest.txt is present, contains exactly the four lines shown above (with a trailing LF) and nothing else.  
• /home/user/support_bundle.tar.gz exists, can be extracted without error, and produces exactly those five files at the root of the archive—no more, no less.

Do not print the contents of any of the files to the console; normal command output is fine.  The whole task can be completed with roughly five to ten standard Linux commands.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent starts):

1. Directory structure
   /home/user/sample-app/
   ├── config.yaml
   ├── env_vars
   ├── logs/
   │   └── app.log
   └── status.txt

2. File contents (all files use LF line endings):

/home/user/sample-app/config.yaml
---------------------------------
app:
  name: Sample App
  version: "1.2.3"
server:
  port: 8080
logging:
  level: INFO
<LF>

/home/user/sample-app/env_vars
------------------------------
PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin
JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/default-java
SAMPLE_APP_OPTS=-Xmx256m
<LF>

/home/user/sample-app/logs/app.log
----------------------------------
2023-10-02 10:40:01 INFO Starting Sample App version 1.2.3
2023-10-02 10:40:05 INFO Listening on port 8080
2023-10-02 10:41:33 WARN Cache miss for key user:42
2023-10-02 10:42:07 ERROR Failed to connect to database
<LF>

/home/user/sample-app/status.txt
--------------------------------
● sample-app.service - Sample App Service
   Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/sample-app.service; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: active (running) since Mon 2023-10-02 10:40:00 UTC; 2h 05min ago
 Main PID: 1234 (java)
    Tasks: 14 (limit: 4915)
   Memory: 150.7M
   CGroup: /system.slice/sample-app.service
           └─1234 /usr/bin/java -jar /opt/sample-app.jar
<LF>

Expected final state after the agent finishes:

A. Directory /home/user/support_bundle exists and contains exactly:
   • app.log               (byte-identical to original)
   • config.yaml           (byte-identical to original)
   • env.txt               (byte-identical to env_vars)
   • service_status.txt    (byte-identical to status.txt)
   • manifest.txt          (4 lines exactly as specified, final LF)

B. File /home/user/support_bundle.tar.gz exists.  
   When extracted (e.g. “tar -xzvf support_bundle.tar.gz -C /tmp/extract”) it yields the five files listed above at archive root and **only** those five files.

No other files or directories should be created outside /home/user/support_bundle or /home/user/support_bundle.tar.gz.
